summary: Variable audience recording—first set muddy, second set much clearer—with a smoking China Cat Sunflower > I Know You Rider and an outstanding Other One.
review: The tape suffers from poor sound on the first set, making it hard to enjoy, but the second set shines with excellent low‑generation quality that lets the band’s 1980 magic come through. Highlights include a ripping China Cat Sunflower > I Know You Rider, a soaring He's Gone tease and what many consider the best Other One of the year. Overall the performance is strong, though the recording inconsistencies keep it from being truly great.

ai_rating.rationale: Show quality is high (standout second set) but recording quality varies greatly; weighted average yields a solid yet not outstanding rating.

recording_quality.technical_notes: First set is muddy and low‑fidelity, making it difficult to listen; second set has noticeably better low‑gen clarity and is easier on the ears, though overall tape quality remains uneven.

standout_songs: China Cat Sunflower > I Know You Rider
show_quality.setlist_flow: First set is musically solid but marred by bad sound; the second set delivers a high‑energy, tour‑de‑force performance that ranks among the year's best shows.
